title: Richly Roasted Ukrainian Pork Casserole
description: A hearty, slow-cooked Ukrainian dish featuring roasted pork, figs, and a rich, savory sauce, perfect for a lazy Sunday dinner. This keto-friendly recipe is made in individual mini cocottes for a satisfying and comforting meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the pork cubes with olive oil, salt, black pepper, and thyme until they are evenly coated.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat and sear the pork cubes until they are browned on all sides.
Transfer the pork to a plate and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and translucent.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional minute.
Add the chopped figs, pork broth, and browned pork to the skillet, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 5 minutes.
Divide the mixture among 4 individual mini cocottes and top each one with a spoonful of sour cream.
Cover the cocottes with lids and transfer them to the preheated oven.
Bake the casseroles for 2 1/2 hours, or until the pork is tender and the sauce is rich and flavorful.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F, then rest 3 minutes before serving.
